key_themes: OpenAI revenue & user-growth miss sparks broad AI/chip selloff (Oracle, AMD, Broadcom, ARM down)
Earnings season split: consumer/financial names (GM, Visa, Starbucks, Coca-Cola, Centene) beat big while Spotify and Robinhood disappoint
Biotech clinical-trial risk: Erasca halved after patient fatality in ERAS-0015 cancer drug trial

stocks.reason: Q1 2026 beat: adj. EPS $3.37 on $49.9B revenue; raised full-year EPS guidance to >$3.40 (from >$3.00), driving shares up ~14.6%
Q1 2026 adj. EPS $3.70 crushed $2.62 estimate; raised full-year EPS guidance to $11.50-$13.50; shares up 5%+
Q2 adj. EPS $3.31 and revenue $11.23B both beat consensus; shares up ~5% after hours
Raised full-year outlook; global and U.S. same-store sales guide lifted to +5% (from +3%); shares up ~5% after hours
Strong Q1 2026 earnings beat; shares rose ~6% to $79.98, one of the top S&P 500 gainers on the day
Q1 MAUs surpassed 760M but Q2 operating income guidance missed; ad-supported revenue -5% YoY; shares fell ~13%
Patient died from treatment-related pneumonitis in ERAS-0015 early-stage cancer trial; shares plunged ~50-55% (record single-day drop)
WSJ report that OpenAI missed revenue and user-growth targets hit Oracle (-4%) given its $300B, 5-year cloud computing partnership with OpenAI
Q1 2026 earnings miss: EPS $0.38 vs $0.43 est., revenue $1.07B vs $1.18B est.; shares down ~6-9% after hours
Q1 2026 beat: revenue $3.18B vs $3.16B est.; Q2 guidance midpoint of $3.45B beat consensus by 5.3%; shares up ~11.4%
Q3 FY2026 earnings beat with better-than-expected outlook; shares surged ~15% after hours on strong data-storage demand
Beat Q1 on EPS ($0.70) and revenue ($4.35B) but Q2 guidance of ~$4.6B missed estimates; shares dropped ~7-8% on valuation concerns
Q1 2026 NOI guidance cut (same-property NOI now -10.5% to -8.5%); year-end occupancy forecast lowered to 86.2-87.8%; shares fell 9-11%
Worst Nasdaq-100 performer (-8%) as investors took profits from AI-driven rally and OpenAI revenue miss fueled broader AI sentiment shift
Beat Q1 estimates on both top and bottom lines; shares moved higher after hours as travel demand remains resilient
